summaryTemplate: {{className}} is the {{patternRole}}. A virtual clone() lets callers copy an object through its base pointer without naming the concrete type. Pattern body lives at {{file}}:{{lineStart}}-{{lineEnd}}.
chosenRationaleTemplate: Tagged as Prototype because {{className}} declares a virtual clone/copy method (lines {{lineStart}}-{{lineEnd}}) that returns a copy of the concrete type behind the base interface.

prototype_clone_method.note: The clone operation. Returning a copy of the dynamic type is what lets callers duplicate without knowing the concrete class.

runnerUpComparisonTemplates.Abstract Factory: Abstract Factory builds new product families from scratch. Prototype produces new objects by copying an existing exemplar — creation is by duplication, not by a factory call.
